purpose: Provide a reusable review module for proactive inspection of system structure and external knowledge quality before defects are handed to diagnostics.

owns: handoff decision to diagnostics, evals, taxonomy loop or content workflow
does_not_own: root-cause diagnosis after a defect is confirmed
final truth of external domain knowledge
responsibility_boundary.handoff_rule: Review identifies observations, risks and confirmed defects. Confirmed systemic defects enter diagnostics; measurable quality checks may enter evals; taxonomy gaps may enter the taxonomy-refinement loop.

review_classes.scope: Routes, information map, module boundaries, closure, schemas, scripts, tools, Canvas coverage, docs boundaries and lifecycle state.
review_classes.default_handoff: diagnostics when a concrete defect or recurrence risk is found

review_classes.scope: Papers, web sources, GitHub topics, claims, evidence chains, recency, source quality, domain placement and uncertainty.
review_classes.default_handoff: knowledge workflow, evals or diagnostics depending on whether the issue is content quality, measurable quality or system structure

review_classes.scope: Canvas scanability, navigation clarity, view boundary, line routing, edge label clearance, visual aesthetics, color semantics, support relation semantics and visual projection completeness.
review_classes.default_handoff: architecture-views or diagnostics when visual blind spots recur

review_classes.scope: Technical documentation, validation coverage, GitHub readiness, setup instructions and operations surfaces.

specialized_review_modules.name: Canvas 视觉审美审查
specialized_review_modules.role: 专门审查 current / active Canvas 图是否具备人眼可读的视觉质量：主流程边是否无标签、节点间距、边标签净空、线条遮挡、导航与正文关系、颜色语义、可见图例、主路径稳定性和视觉投影完整性。
owns: edge label clearance review for exceptional labeled edges
line obstruction and node overlap review
specialized_review_modules.handoff_rule: If a visual finding is a confirmed layout or policy defect with recurrence risk, record the review finding and hand it to diagnostics for owner-layer repair; local one-off drawing polish can stay in architecture-views.
key_review_steps: load Canvas registry, view graph and affected .canvas files
run geometry/structure visual audit with scripts/audit-canvas-visual.ps1 -Strict
run screenshot evidence audit with scripts/audit-canvas-screenshot.ps1 -Strict
inspect generated PNG/report before accepting user-visible visual quality
handoff recurring visual gate defects to diagnostics
check that non-navigation support file labels have support_bindings to upper Canvas content nodes
treat missing support semantics as a visual blind spot because the bottom machine-truth band becomes loose bibliography

specialized_review_modules.name: Git 版本与发布审查
specialized_review_modules.role: 专门审查本地版本边界、提交范围、敏感信息、远端授权和 GitHub 公开前准备；它判断发布风险，不替代 Git 治理模块维护状态。
owns: sensitive surface review before staging or publication
does_not_own: root-cause diagnosis after failed Git workflow
specialized_review_modules.handoff_rule: If review finds a concrete Git workflow defect, privacy leak risk, validation gap or repeated publication-boundary failure, hand off to diagnostics with git status snapshot and changed paths.

finding_status_values.meaning: Something worth noting, but no immediate defect or action is confirmed.
A plausible weakness that may become a defect if volume, reuse or dependency grows.
A confirmed mismatch, missing surface, false claim, stale source, invalid route or broken contract.
Review cannot decide without missing evidence, access or user choice.

handoff_targets.rule: Use when review finds no actionable defect.
Use when a finding has a concrete failure path, system owner layer or recurrence risk that needs root-cause repair.
Use when the finding should become a measurable quality test or benchmark.
Use when the finding is mainly a classification gap or domain-routing ambiguity.
Use when the finding is a content update, source-quality note, claim uncertainty or synthesis issue.

core_workflow.rule: Name what is being reviewed, why now, and which surfaces are in or out of scope.
Read route registry, information map, relevant module registries, source records, schemas, docs maps, Canvas registry or catalog entries before judging.
Choose criteria appropriate to the review class instead of using one generic checklist for all reviews.

For visual_review of current/active Canvas layout, navigation, color, readability or user-visible defects, run scripts/audit-canvas-visual.ps1 -Strict and scripts/audit-canvas-screenshot.ps1 -Strict before classifying final findings.
Classify each finding as observation, risk, defect or blocked. Do not diagnose root cause inside review unless the defect path is already explicit.
Send confirmed systemic defects to diagnostics; send measurable quality gaps to evals; send taxonomy gaps to the taxonomy loop; keep non-actionable observations in the review record.
Capture scope, evidence, criteria, findings, risk assessment, handoff decision and acceptance before claiming the review is complete.
Run review validation and any route-specific validators required by the reviewed surfaces.
Confirm the review answered the original review question and routed defects to the correct downstream module.

criteria: module is registered in information-map
route has read, update and validation surfaces
canonical truth is machine-readable
schema or contract exists when structured records exist
validator can catch drift
closure map records lifecycle and gaps
visual coverage decision exists
docs remain thin and routed
module owner_layer and module_kind match system/module-layer-model.json
module layer, storage path, lifecycle and Canvas view_level are not conflated
new stable module placement has a system-structure-governance decision before route, closure or Canvas registration
module-relationship-map covers the module and its key relationships
new or upgraded system modules audit and inherit existing assets in their responsibility boundary
modules listed in module_activation_contract.current_required_modules have closure-map activation_proof
diagnostic-system self-audit exists when user correction proves a missed or shallow diagnosis
system upgrades include same-class impact scan before closure
system upgrades separate incident_manifestation from root_problem and name system_regulation_target before closure
agent, skill and module entrypoint docs stay thin and route to machine truth
module-specific methods live in registered route, docs map, registry, schema or workflow surfaces
source is traceable
claim is separated from evidence
recency and version are named when unstable
uncertainty and conflicts are recorded
domain classification reaches level 3 when applicable
copyright-sensitive content is summarized rather than copied
source quality or methodological limits are visible
knowledge item can be indexed and retrieved
main structure stays left and navigation stays right
navigation file buttons are compact
nav labels explain the destination
bridge lines mean correspondence only
lines do not cross unrelated nodes
edge labels have enough whitespace and are not hidden by adjacent cards
view boundary is registered
Canvas points back to machine truth
L0 navigation grouping follows registered view_level, layer and primary_subject
L2 system-subsystem views are visually and graph-wise nested under L1 system overview, not peers of it
visual fixes scan peer current/active Canvas views for the same failure class
current/active Canvas visual changes have screenshot-level evidence when user-visible layout or readability is affected
primary reading path has one dominant direction
L0 navigation index reads as a layer-aware route map, not a subjective mixed bucket list or plain file-preview stack
navigation file buttons stay visually subordinate to human-readable destination cards
support files use file-label-* as integrated readable cards with compact embedded file buttons
primary-flow edges are unlabeled by default
exceptional edge labels are readable and do not overlap node borders
lines do not cross unrelated cards, labels, file nodes or section bands
navigation panel stays visually separate from the main structure
navigation-to-content bridge lines use one explanation and one direct line
machine truth file nodes stay in a separate band
scripts/audit-canvas-visual.ps1 -Strict passes before visual acceptance
entry navigation cards expose L1/L2 ownership before opening child views
entry and overview views do not confuse Canvas view_level with module owner_layer
L0 entry exposes L1 trunks first and nests L2 system-subsystem shortcuts under the L1 system overview
view-scoped color_semantics exists for every current or active Canvas view
visible color-legend nodes explain the active palette without becoming machine truth
taxonomy/domain colors are not interpreted as system status colors
layer guide lines remain distinct from workflow/dataflow edges
Canvas colors use registered view-scoped palettes and stable Obsidian color tokens 1-6 or #RRGGBB; plain named colors are treated as a style defect.

Review acceptance separates static audit results from user-visible visual acceptance; if rendered Obsidian inspection is unavailable, the limitation is stated instead of pretending full visual certainty.
scripts/audit-canvas-screenshot.ps1 -Strict generates PNG evidence and system/reviews/canvas-screenshots/latest-report.json before screenshot-level visual acceptance
screenshot evidence is treated as review evidence, while Canvas registry and view graph remain machine truth
Screenshot-level visual audit is a key step of Canvas visual review, not an optional artifact-generation step.
origin remote points to the registered BoundlessKnowledge repository
no secrets, tokens, cookies, local workspace state or personal private material are included in the intended version surface
changed paths have a readable commit scope and do not mix unrelated system, knowledge and generated artifacts
required validators passed or failures are explicitly diagnosed before commit or publication
remote actions are not performed without explicit user approval
GitHub readiness document and Git governance machine truth agree on publication boundaries

quality_gates: Review must name scope before judging quality.
Review must read the authoritative machine surfaces for the selected scope.
Review must distinguish observation, risk, defect and blocked evidence.
Review must not replace diagnostics when a confirmed defect needs root-cause repair.
External knowledge review must preserve source traceability and uncertainty.
System review must check whether visual projection, closure and validation surfaces need updates.

Canvas visual aesthetic review must run scripts/audit-canvas-visual.ps1 -Strict for current or active Canvas layout changes and treat primary-flow edge labels or edge-label obstruction as visual defects.
Canvas visual aesthetic review must check that L0 entry navigation groups destinations by registered view_level/layer/primary_subject, not only by visual neatness.
System review must check module-layer-model alignment when module boundaries, information-map, closure map or entry Canvas views change.
System review must check system-structure-governance and module-relationship-map when module boundaries, ownership, branch decisions or new stable modules change.
System review must check legacy_asset_audit when a new system module, child module, peer module or branch is introduced.
System review must check activation_proof before accepting a new or upgraded stable system module as active/effective.
Canvas entry and view graph review must reject L2 system-subsystem views being parented directly by L0 or shown as peers of L1 system overview.
System review must check diagnostic_self_audit when a user correction reveals that diagnostics missed the true owner layer or accepted closure too early.
System review must reject target-only system_upgrade closure when same-class assets and sibling surfaces were not scanned.
Review handoff to diagnostics must mention whether the finding appears isolated, repeated across same-class assets, or requires a generalization_scan.

System review must reject a system_upgrade that treats an observed accident, screenshot, graph defect or file-specific failure as the root problem without naming recurrence mechanism and regulation target.
System review must reject entrypoint documents that carry module-specific workflow, output template, schema field list or hidden local method instead of routing to machine truth.

Canvas visual review must run scripts/audit-canvas-screenshot.ps1 -Strict for current or active Canvas visual changes that involve layout, navigation, color, readability or user-visible visual defects.
Canvas screenshot audit is a key review step: do not accept current/active Canvas visual review when screenshot evidence is skipped without an explicit limitation record.
Git/version publication review must run before any public remote action when the project is being prepared for GitHub publication.
